Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 262 upvotes, 54 comments
  2. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 55 upvotes, 8 comments
  3. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 298 upvotes, 71 comments
  4. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 1174 upvotes, 336 comments
  5. Hardware Image Compression 52 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 109 upvotes, 31 comments
  7. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 633 upvotes, 237 comments
  8. VHDL's Crown Jewel 111 upvotes, 38 comments
  9. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 131 upvotes, 47 comments
  10. Comprehensive C++ Hashmap Benchmarks (2022) 40 upvotes, 11 comments
  11. How to turn anything into a router 224 upvotes, 97 comments
  12. I use excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 197 upvotes, 87 comments
  13. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 65 upvotes, 24 comments
  14. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 362 upvotes, 180 comments
  15. FTC Action Against Match and OkCupid for Deceiving Users, Sharing Personal Data 42 upvotes, 14 comments
  16. "Over 1.5 million GitHub PRs have had ads injected into them by Copilot" 155 upvotes, 78 comments
  17. Parrots pack twice as many neurons as primate brains of the same mass 163 upvotes, 88 comments
  18. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 871 upvotes, 568 comments
  19. From Proxmox to FreeBSD and Sylve in Our Office Lab 16 upvotes, 5 comments
  20. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 28 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. 72% of the dollar's purchasing power was destroyed in just four episodes 100 upvotes, 63 comments
  22. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 32 upvotes, 17 comments
  23. Spring Boot Done Right: Lessons from a 400-Module Codebase 56 upvotes, 41 comments
  24. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 292 upvotes, 307 comments
  25. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 76 upvotes, 71 comments
  26. In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? 38 upvotes, 32 comments
  27. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 316 upvotes, 387 comments
  28. The First Video Game Was Just a Box in the Corner of a Bar 29 upvotes, 27 comments
  29. Bitwarden Integrates with OneCLI Agent Vault 10 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. How Reverse Game Theory Could Solve the Housing Shortage 28 upvotes, 38 comments
